title: Nyquist Sampling Theorem
content: A continuous signal must be sampled at a rate at least twice its highest frequency component to be perfectly reconstructed: f_s ≥ 2·f_max.
extended_content: Sampling below the Nyquist rate causes aliasing — high-frequency components fold back and corrupt lower-frequency content.  Anti-aliasing low-pass filters are applied before sampling to remove frequencies above f_s / 2 (the Nyquist frequency).
source: Oppenheim & Schafer, Discrete-Time Signal Processing, 3rd ed.
